examples.title: Algorithm: Linked Node Structure
examples.description: Fundamental structure for linked representation of binary tree nodes
examples.code:
ALGORITHM CreateNode(value)
BEGIN
 CREATE node
 node.value ← value
 node.left ← NULL
 node.right ← NULL
 RETURN node
END
examples.explanation: Creates a node with given value and initializes child pointers to null. This forms the basic building block for linked tree structures.

examples.title: C Implementation of Binary Tree Node
examples.description: Dynamic memory allocation for linked representation
examples.explanation: Uses struct for node definition and malloc for dynamic allocation. Left/right pointers maintain tree structure.

examples.title: Algorithm: Array Representation
examples.description: Storing complete binary tree in array
examples.explanation: Demonstrates index relationships for array storage. Suitable for complete trees to avoid space waste.

examples.title: Array-Based Binary Tree Class
examples.explanation: Implements array storage with index-based child access methods. Handles incomplete trees by checking array bounds.

examples.title: Complete Tree Construction
examples.description: Building a sample binary tree using linked nodes
examples.explanation: Demonstrates hierarchical construction of tree nodes. Shows typical Java implementation pattern.
